name: eval-1-architect-from-description
query: I need a subagent that reviews Postgres migrations before they are applied. It should auto-delegate when I'm working with migration files and dry-run the migration to check for safety issues.
expected_behavior: Activates agent-architect in Architect mode (not Lock or Push)
Runs the pre-flight check and confirms a custom subagent is the right tool (not CLAUDE.md, not slash command, not built-in Explore/Plan)
Asks at most 3 scoping questions, one per turn, skipping questions already answered by the user's request
Generated frontmatter includes name (kebab-case), description with MUST BE USED or PROACTIVELY directive, tools list at least-privilege (Read, Glob, Grep, Bash for dry-run), model (sonnet or inherit)

Generated body includes all required sections: When invoked, Process (numbered steps), Output format (specific deliverable structure), Boundaries (Do NOT items), Report/Response (Status, Summary, Findings, Obstacles, Next steps)
Runs Step 6 self-validation against the anti-pattern catalog before writing the file
Asks for deployment scope (project vs. user) before writing
After writing, surfaces the reload reminder (run /agents) and a suggested test invocation
Does not include time-sensitive caveats or vendor-specific marketing in the generated file
failure_modes_to_check: Description is vague ('helps with migrations') instead of directive
Body assumes chat history (stateful assumption anti-pattern)
